findings.title: ASMR-Bench: Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research
findings.abstract: As AI systems are increasingly used to conduct research autonomously, misaligned systems could introduce subtle flaws that produce misleading results while evading detection. We introduce ASMR-Bench (Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research), a benchmark for evaluating the ability of auditors to detect

findings.title: Beyond Distribution Sharpening: The Importance of Task Rewards

findings.title: Characterising LLM-Generated Competency Questions: a Cross-Domain Empirical Study using Open and Closed Models

findings.title: ArtifactNet: Detecting AI-Generated Music via Forensic Residual Physics
findings.abstract: We present ArtifactNet, a lightweight framework that detects AI-generated music by reframing the problem as forensic physics -- extracting and analyzing the physical artifacts that neural audio codecs inevitably imprint on generated audio. A bounded-mask UNet (ArtifactUNet, 3.6M parameters) extracts

findings.title: Enhancing AI and Dynamical Subseasonal Forecasts with Probabilistic Bias Correction
findings.abstract: Decision-makers rely on weather forecasts to plant crops, manage wildfires, allocate water and energy, and prepare for weather extremes. findings.title: Why Open Source? A Game-Theoretic Analysis of the AI Race
findings.abstract: In recent years, with the advancement of frontier AI, we have observed certain dynamics in open-sourcing and closed-sourcing decisions. findings.abstract: Computational X-ray absorption near-edge structure (XANES) is widely used to probe local coordination environments, oxidation states, and electronic structure in chemically complex systems. findings.title: Real-Time Solution-Seeking for Game-Theoretic Autonomous Driving via Time-Distributed Iterations
findings.abstract: Computational complexity has been a major challenge in game-theoretic model predictive control (GT-MPC), as real-time solutions to a game (e.g., Nash equilibria (NEs)) have to be computed at each sampling instant of an MPC. findings.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation

findings.title: Spinning Living Crystals of Run-and-Tumble Particles with Environmental Feedback
findings.abstract: Collective rotations are common in active matter, enhancing cohesion, transport, and mixing. findings.title: Compositional Design, Implementation, and Verification of Swarms (Technical Report)
